name: OpenHands
description: Open-source autonomous agent platform (formerly OpenDevin) that completes whole engineering tasks across a codebase via CLI and web.

developer.comment: Built by All Hands AI; project formerly named OpenDevin.

first-release.comment: Launched March 2024 as OpenDevin; rebranded to OpenHands in late 2024. Date is approximate first public release of the project.

license.comment: Core is MIT-licensed; the enterprise directory has separate licensing.

specialization.comment: Broad autonomous coding agent for end-to-end engineering tasks.

interface.comment: Ships a CLI, a local React web GUI, and a hosted cloud web app; also a Python SDK.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal (CLI) and browser (web GUI / cloud); not embedded in a host IDE.

open-source.comment: Core agent is MIT-licensed and open source.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Self-contained CLI/SDK plus a hosted OpenHands Cloud service.

default-model-provider.comment: Anthropic Claude is the recommended default; model-agnostic with many providers supported.

model-choice.comment: Model-agnostic; connects to any LLM via OpenRouter, direct API keys, or local models.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYO LLM key supported in both open-source and Cloud (available to all Cloud users since Nov 2025).

multi-file-editing.comment: Agents plan, write, and apply changes across the codebase.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Receives a task, plans, then executes steps (edit/run/fix) in a sandboxed Docker container end-to-end.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs shell commands and builds/tests inside a sandboxed Docker environment.

mcp-support.comment: First-class Model Context Protocol support with OAuth flows, tool filtering, and configurable timeouts.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: SDK supports hierarchical sub-agent delegation; sub-agents run as independent conversations enabling structured parallelism.

codebase-indexing.comment: Reads files on demand into context rather than building a semantic embedding index by default; persistent indexing only via external MCP tools (e.g. Codebase-Memory).

planning-mode.comment: Planning Mode (beta, landed v1.6.0 Mar 2026) creates a detailed plan and asks for approval before writing code.

browser-computer-use.comment: Agents can use the web/browser as part of their action space.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: DelegateTool exposes spawn/delegate to the LLM, so the agent itself decides mid-task when to fan out work to independent sub-agents (model-driven, parallel via threads). Caveat: the delegation tool is opt-in — the developer must add it to the agent's tool list; it is not enabled by default. Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive/supervised agent locally; autonomous and async/background runs via Cloud (e.g. GitHub issue to PR).

inline-completion.comment: Agent-driven, not a tab-autocomplete tool; no as-you-type inline completion.

background-async-execution.comment: OpenHands Cloud runs tasks asynchronously (e.g. dispatched from a GitHub issue) while the user does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: Repository-resident AGENTS.md and skills provide cross-session project context; the agent itself does not auto-write durable memory by default.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No built-in checkpoint/snapshot-restore mechanism documented; runs occur in a Docker sandbox and rely on git. Could not confirm a one-click rollback feature as of 2026-06-05.

git-integration.comment: Native git integrations; can work from issues and produce commits/PRs.

pr-creation.comment: Can read a GitHub issue, work on a fix, and open a pull request.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (plus model variants CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md) as repository-wide always-on instructions.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Composable/extensible Software Agent SDK, MCP tool integration, and a skills mechanism.

permission-model.comment: Actions execute inside a sandboxed Docker container; autonomy/approval is configurable (Planning Mode can require approval before writing code).

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open source runs locally; Enterprise self-hosts in your VPC via Kubernetes.

privacy-mode.comment: Self-hosted/VPC deployment guarantees code never leaves your environment; running locally or self-hosted avoids sending code to the vendor.

enterprise-controls.comment: Enterprise offers SAML/SSO, fine-grained RBAC, complete audit logs, and VPC self-hosting.

pricing-model.comment: Free open source (MIT), free Cloud Individual tier, paid pay-as-you-go usage, $500/mo Growth subscription, BYO LLM key option.

free-tier.comment: Fully free open-source self-host plus a free Cloud Individual tier (up to 10 daily conversations).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest fixed monthly plan is Cloud Growth at $500/mo (unlimited users, RBAC). Individual paid use is pay-as-you-go (usage-based, no fixed plan). Checked 2026-06-05.

maturity.comment: 75k+ GitHub stars, 490+ contributors, commercial Cloud/Enterprise offerings; widely deployed though still rapidly evolving (Planning Mode in beta).

community-size.comment: 75.9k+ GitHub stars, 490+ contributors, active Slack community; leading open-source coding agent.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server ecosystem plus extensible SDK and shareable skills.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (recommended) and model-specific variants like CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md as always-on repo instructions; legacy .openhands/microagents is deprecated.

memory-scopes.comment: Skills/instructions can be defined at user level (~/.agents/skills) and repository/project level (.agents/skills, AGENTS.md).

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is always-on; skills are keyword-triggered/agent-requested via progressive disclosure rather than path/file-type globs.

auto-memory.comment: No documented automatic cross-session memory the agent writes on its own; relies on manual AGENTS.md/skills. Checked 2026-06-05.

skill-support.comment: Implements the AgentSkills standard (SKILL.md in .agents/skills/) — reusable on-demand capabilities with supporting scripts/resources.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills activate via keyword triggers in user prompts and are also agent-invoked autonomously when relevant.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: the agent sees a summary first and reads full skill content only when needed.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are markdown files dropped into .agents/skills/ (repo or user), shared via git; no curated marketplace/registry documented.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Default runtime executes all agent actions inside a Docker container (base image nikolaik/python-nodejs); pluggable remote runtimes (Daytona, E2B, Modal) provide microVM-class isolation. A local-process runtime exists for development but is unsandboxed.

permission-granularity.comment: Gating is per-action: confirmation policies pause on each action (AlwaysConfirm) or only risk-flagged ones (ConfirmRisky via the security analyzer). No command-prefix allowlists, per-path write scoping, or read/write distinction documented.

rule-precedence.comment: No declarative allow/deny rule engine; gating is driven by confirmation policies and risk-based security analyzers (LLM, pattern, policy-rail), not user-authored allow/deny rules with precedence.

permission-configurability.comment: SDK exposes programmable decision points: custom confirmation handlers (poll agent_status, reject_pending_actions), pluggable/custom security analyzers (Pattern, PolicyRail, Ensemble), and a security_policy_filename Jinja2 policy template.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No default deny patterns for .env/secrets documented; protection relies on confirmation policy or a user-authored security analyzer/policy. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: Confirmation is decided per action via policy; no documented 'always allow this command' rule saved to durable config to suppress future prompts. LLM/config settings persist to ~/.openhands/settings.json, but granular per-command approvals do not. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: CLI --always-approve (alias --yolo) auto-approves all actions; headless mode always runs in always-approve. SDK NeverConfirm() policy executes all actions without approval.

network-access-control.comment: No default outbound egress blocking documented; the sandbox container has network access out of the box. Networking is configurable (e.g. use_host_network, loopback binding) but not an enforced allowlist by default. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Documented default is 'Always ask for confirmation' on each action (AlwaysConfirm) before edits and commands; auto-approval is opt-in via --always-approve or headless mode.
